finding.vuln_claim: SQL injection via unsanitized `direction` parameter injected directly into ORDER BY clause
SSRF via broken allowlist check
Path traversal via incomplete sanitization; replacing only '../' allows bypass with '....//'.
Unsafe deserialization of attacker-controlled cookie via pickle.loads
Mass assignment via Object.assign allows attacker to overwrite any user field including role/isAdmin
IDOR: auth check only blocks if BOTH id mismatch AND not support; any authenticated user can modify other users
No validation of req.body contents before merging into user object; allows setting arbitrary fields including internal DB fields
OS command injection via unsanitized `name` parameter injected into shell command string
Path traversal in `src`: startswith check bypassed with /var/data/../../etc/passwd
Stored XSS via unsanitized markdown rendering stored directly as HTML
Race condition (TOCTOU) in balance check-then-update allows double-spend
No validation that amount is positive allows negative transfers to steal funds
'to' field passed unsanitized to db calls may enable SQL injection if db methods interpolate input
Failed signature verification silently falls back to unverified jwt.decode() in production.
Timing side-channel in HMAC comparison via non-constant-time string equality
Open redirect via protocol-relative URL bypass
XXE via lxml XMLParser with resolve_entities=True and no_network=False allows external entity injection
SSRF via XML external entity with no_network=False allows the parser to make outbound HTTP/FTP requests
NoSQL injection via unsanitized req.body passed directly as query object
SSRF via user-supplied URL with only scheme check
gets() writes unbounded input into a 64-byte stack buffer, enabling stack-based buffer overflow.
Sensitive token stored in plaintext SharedPreferences readable on rooted devices or via backup.
Hardcoded AES key in source code
Null pointer dereference guard is inverted; condition `out || out_sz==0` passes when out is NULL, allowing memcpy to write to NULL.

SSRF via broken allowlist check
Path traversal via incomplete sanitization of '../' sequences

IDOR: the auth check compares req.user.id to nothing — missing equality check against req.params.id lets any user edit any account.
No input validation on req.body before merging; attacker can inject prototype-polluting or unexpected fields.

Path traversal bypass: `src` prefix check is bypassable with `/var/data/../../../etc/shadow` to exfiltrate arbitrary files
Stored XSS via unsanitized markdown-rendered HTML persisted to DB and returned to clients
Race condition (TOCTOU) between balance read and write enables double-spend.
No validation that amount is positive; negative or zero values reverse or skip the transfer.
Recipient account 'to' is never validated to exist or belong to a real user, enabling balance injection to arbitrary IDs.
JWT signature verification bypassed in production on any verify failure
Timing side-channel via non-constant-time string comparison
Open redirect via protocol-relative URL bypass

gets() writes unbounded input into 64-byte stack buffer, enabling stack-based buffer overflow.
Sensitive token stored in plaintext SharedPreferences, readable on rooted devices or via adb backup.
Hardcoded AES key exposed in source code
Guard condition is inverted: checks `out` (non-NULL) instead of `!out`, so null pointer is accepted and NULL dereference occurs on line 5-6.

SSRF via insufficient URL allowlist check
Open redirect / response proxying with no output sanitization allows content injection
IDOR via unsanitized inv_id before authorization check

Unsafe deserialization of attacker-controlled cookie via pickle.loads
Mass assignment allows attacker to overwrite any user field including privileged ones (e.g. isAdmin, role).
Authorization check only blocks if BOTH conditions fail; authenticated non-support users can modify any other user's record.
No validation that req.params.id references an existing user; a null/undefined user object passed to Object.assign causes unhandled exception or prototype pollution.

Stored XSS via unsanitized markdown-rendered HTML persisted to DB and returned to clients.
Race condition (TOCTOU) allows double-spend via concurrent requests.
No validation on amount allows negative or zero values enabling reverse transfers.

jwt.decode() bypasses signature verification in production on any JWT error

OAuth token stored in SharedPreferences plaintext, readable by rooted devices or backup extraction.
Hardcoded AES key in source code
NULL pointer dereference due to inverted guard: `out ||` should be `!out ||`, allowing NULL out to pass the check.
SQL injection via unsanitized `direction` parameter injected directly into ORDER BY clause
SSRF via broken allowlist check — the condition on line 3 is syntactically malformed (regex literal `/`/ truncated), so the guard never executes and any URL is fetched.
Unvalidated URL parameter passed directly to axios; even a repaired allowlist can be bypassed via redirects or DNS rebinding since no redirect policy is set.
IDOR: inv_id is fetched before authorization; if db.get_invoice raises no error on missing/other-tenant invoices, the 403 check may be bypassable or leak existence.
No re-validation of note content allows mass-assignment or stored XSS if inv.to_json() is rendered as HTML without escaping.
Path traversal via single-pass replace allows directory escape
Unsafe deserialization of attacker-controlled cookie via pickle.loads allows arbitrary code execution.
Mass assignment via Object.assign allows overwriting any user field including role/isAdmin/isSupport from req.body.
Authorization check uses || instead of && (likely syntax bug), so any support user can modify any other user's account.
No existence check after findById; if user is null, Object.assign(null, req.body) throws and may leak stack trace.
OS command injection via unsanitized `name` parameter injected into shell command string
Path traversal via `src` bypass using symlinks or encoded paths that satisfy startsWith('/var/data/') check
Stored XSS via unsanitized markdown rendering stored and returned as raw HTML
Race condition (TOCTOU) in balance check-then-update allows double-spend.
No input validation on 'to' or 'amount' fields; negative amount enables reverse transfer (credit self).
No validation that 'to' is a valid/existing user; funds sent to nonexistent account are created out of thin air.
JWT verification failure silently falls back to unverified decode in production
Insecure Direct Object Reference: authenticated users can enumerate any user record by supplying arbitrary uid values beyond their own.
Timing side-channel: string equality `==` leaks signature length/content via timing differences.
Open redirect via protocol-relative URL bypassing startswith('/') check
XXE via resolve_entities=True and no_network=False allows external entity injection
NoSQL injection via unsanitized req.body fields passed directly as MongoDB query object
SSRF via unvalidated URL parameter allows requests to internal network resources
Insufficient URL validation allows http/https bypass via schemes like http.evil.com or redirect chains to file://
gets() writes unbounded user input into a 64-byte stack buffer, enabling stack-based buffer overflow.
Auth token stored in SharedPreferences (MODE_PRIVATE) is readable on rooted devices and via ADB backup.
Hardcoded AES key embedded in source code
os.urandom used without import; if missing, IV generation silently fails or raises NameError
Guard condition is inverted: checks `out` (non-NULL) instead of `!out`, so null pointer is accepted and NULL dereference occurs on line 5-6.
